title: Asplenium woodwardioides

intro: Asplenium woodwardioides, also known as the golden spleenwort, is a type of fern that belongs to the Aspleniaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Asplenium woodwardioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Asplenium woodwardioides is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
